zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Pershale於大約1322年出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Adam de Peshale II47歲，母親Joan de Eyton24歲。他於1340年與Joan Chetwynd在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1387年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾可利享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Pershale于大约1322年出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Adam de Peshale II47岁，母亲Joan de Eyton24岁。他于1340年与Joan Chetwynd在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1387年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾可利享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pershale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Perschall, a variant of Parschall (see Parshall ); alternatively, a variant of Perschel, from a pet form of Persch, a short form of Berthold or Peter in the border area with Czechia.

variants.name: Parshall
meanings: Americanized form of South German Parschall, a variant of Parschalk, a status name for a freeholder (a freeman with few service obligations), from Middle High German bar ‘naked’, ‘free from, apparent’ + schalk ‘servant, man’. See Pershall .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Pershale was born about 1322, in Shropshire, England, his father, Adam de Peshale II, was 47 and his mother, Joan de Eyton, was 24. He married Joan Chetwynd in 1340, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1387, in Checkley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Pershale est né environ 1322, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam de Peshale II, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Joan d’Eyton, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Joan Chetwynd en 1340, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1387, à Checkley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Pershale um 1322 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam de Peshale II 47 und seine Mutter Joan de Eyton 24. Er hat 1340 in Shropshire, England Joan Chetwynd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1387 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Checkley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Pershale è nato il circa 1322 nel Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Adam de Peshale II, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Eyton, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Joan Chetwynd nel 1340, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1387, nel Checkley, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Pershaleが約1322年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Adam de Peshale IIは47歳，母Joan de Eytonは24歳であった。1340年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでJoan Chetwyndと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1387年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアチェックリーで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Pershale(이)가 대략 1322년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam de Peshale II(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Eyton(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1340년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Joan Chetwynd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1387년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 체클리에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Pershale nasceu aproximadamente 1322, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam de Peshale II, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Eyton, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Chetwynd em 1340, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1387, em Checkley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Pershale родился около 1322 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Adam de Peshale II было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Eyton, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Chetwynd в 1340 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1387 г. в местности чеккли, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 66 лет.

sm.from 1377 to 1385: mai le 1377 e o'o I le 1385

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Pershale nació aproximadamente 1322, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam de Peshale II, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Joan de Eyton, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Joan Chetwynd en 1340, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1387, en Checkley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 66 años.
